question.text: Turnover for exchange-traded derivatives is computed as:

answer.text: Total of favourable and unfavourable differences, plus premium received on sale of options
Total contract value of all trades
Gross buy value plus gross sell value

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Turnover = total of favorable differences + unfavorable differences + premium received on option sales + any reverse trade settlement amounts. This is per ICAI Guidance Note on Tax Audit. **The Trap:** Turnover is NOT the contract value — it's the sum of absolute P&L amounts. **Cross-Exam Context:** Turnover computation is tested in Series I. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This turnover figure is crucial for determining whether tax audit under Section 44AB is applicable.
